steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice emphasizes the order in which the two lighthouse keepers began their careers. The notes show Moore started in 1817 and Hecox started in 1883. The correct choice must make that chronological sequence explicit.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Catherine A. Moore's career as a lighthouse keeper ended in 1878, whereas Laura J. Hecox's ended in 1917.’: addresses when their careers ended, not when they began. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Laura J. Hecox began her career as a lighthouse keeper years after Catherine A. Moore did.’: directly establishes the start sequence using the data (Moore 1817, Hecox 1883), making clear who came first. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘From 1817 to 1878, the nighttime waters of Connecticut were more navigable thanks to lighthouse keepers Laura J. Hecox and Catherine A. Moore.’: incorrectly places Hecox in Connecticut during 1817–1878, which misreads the notes. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Before automation, lighthouse keepers like Catherine A. Moore and Laura J. Hecox were crucial to ensuring safe navigation for watercraft.’: makes a general statement about lighthouse keepers' importance without addressing when either career started. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
